prevalence.value: Low in general population; concentrated in historically exposed worker cohorts
prevalence.population: Adults with past occupational asbestos exposure
prevalence.source: WHO Europe asbestos-related disease context; occupational cohort syntheses
prevalence.trend: declining in new exposures; persistent burden due to long latency

incidence.value: Low; cases occur decades after exposure

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; high in affected cohorts with progressive fibrosis
burden.mortalityRate: Moderate in severe cases; related asbestos malignancy risk persists

vulnerablePopulations: workers in informal renovation without adequate protection
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Exposure history plus HRCT findings of interstitial fibrosis and pleural plaques; restrictive spirometry; exclude other ILDs

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: occupational surveillance varies by country and industry; not population-wide
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of thousands across EU exposed cohorts (uncertain)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Limited (irreversible fibrosis); focus on supportive care and prevention of complications

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Pulmonology and occupational medicine services widely available; HRCT access is generally good
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Smoking cessation, vaccinations, pulmonary rehab, oxygen when needed, manage comorbid COPD; malignancy vigilance as clinically indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Managing long-term cohort follow-up and comorbidities in aging exposed populations
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong occupational health frameworks and diagnostic capacity; prevention policies reduce future burden
